warnings: fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose: Fukui reactivity indices were computed for furan (9 atoms: O, C, C, C, C, H, H, H, H) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N±1 states (cation: charge +1, mult 2; anion: charge −1, mult 2) were computed via finite differences. Partial charges were obtained via the Mulliken scheme. The geometry was taken from the supplied furan.xyz as-is (no re-optimization). The headline value reported is the maximum f⁺ (electrophilic Fukui index), 1.9242931, located at atom 5 (C, an α-carbon). Charge conservation checks passed: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000 and sum(f-) = 1.0000. Integrity status: trustworthy = true. The three integrity checks reported by the engine are reproduced verbatim in the warnings field. Note: Mulliken-based Fukui indices can yield negative values on electronegative atoms (O) and hydrogens due to basis-set overlap artifacts — a known limitation of the Mulliken finite-difference scheme.
